channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Deep tech disruption: ecco come le tecnologie avanzate stanno trasformando le aziende
item.description: I CEO, i CIO e i CFO stanno scoprendo che la “deep tech” sta guidando attivamente l’innovazione aziendale e la redditività.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Tech workers in Kenya hold vigil for TikTok content moderator who died
item.description: Technology workers in Kenya have held a vigil for a colleague who died in unclear circumstances after she was unable to travel to her home in Nigeria for two years.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: I’m a tech pro and these are my secrets to better flights and luxury travel
item.description: Here are some travel hacks to save money on your next vacation including setting up price alerts, remaining flexible and checking for 24-hour price drops.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Google reportedly looking at smart glasses
item.description: After abandoning its Google Glass smart glasses in 2023, Google seems ready to take another kick at the can with what will reportedly be a $115 million acquisition of Canadian startup AdHawk Microsystems.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Creating a cybersecurity standard of care: The future of software liability
item.description: This approach emphasizes the importance of integrating security measures into the software development lifecycle from the beginning. The post Creating a cybersecurity standard of care: The future of software liability first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Chinese cyberespionage group deploys custom backdoors on Juniper routers
item.description: A Chinese cyberespionage group with a history of exploiting proprietary network-edge devices and developing custom malware for them has also been targeting enterprise and ISP-grade Juniper MX Series routers, according to a report by Google’s Mandiant team.
item.dc:creator: csoonline

item.title: GTA Online publisher Take-Two is gunning for a black market that’s basically heaven for cheaters
item.description: Take-Two is going after online marketplace PlayerAuctionsfor allegedly selling hacked GTA Online accounts and currency.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Box CEO Aaron Levie finds a middle ground on tech policy during Trump’s second term
item.description: In our current political and media environment the loudest voices are the ones that are farthest from the center. Tech hasn’t been spared, with some Silicon Valley leaders drifting right—not only out of ideology, but also pragmatism.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Cato Networks unveils next-gen firewall
item.description: Cato Networks this week introduced its next-generation firewall as part of its secure access service edge (SASE) platform, saying the NGFW would mitigate security vulnerabilities left unpatched by manual efforts.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Malware steals bank cards and passwords from millions of devices
item.description: One report claims at least 25 million people were targeted by infostealer malware between the beginning of 2023 and the end of 2024, capturing bank card details and passwords.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: French publishers and authors sue Meta over copyright works used in AI training
item.description: French publishers and authors said Wednesday they're taking Meta to court, accusing the social media company of using their works without permission to train its artificial intelligence model.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: ServiceNow’s Yokohama platform release focuses on agentic AI
item.description: ServiceNow today launched its newest Now Platform release, Yokohama, which doubles down on the company’s commitment to agentic AI.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Big Rivian update delivers hands-off driving to rival Tesla Autopilot –and a new 'Rally' mode
item.description: Rivian's software update brings greater levels of autonomous driving, as well as some new convenience features.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: FinOps sale de las fronteras de la nube
item.description: El año pasado, FinOps se generalizó en el panorama empresarial y, según IDC, el 75% de las empresas de la lista Global 2000 de Forbes ya lo han adoptado.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: ChatGPT wants to write your next novel, and readers and writers alike should be very worried
item.description: Sam Altman shows off a new ChatGPT model that's very good at creative writing, as long as you want robot-written fiction.
item.dc:creator: john-anthony.disotto@futurenet.com (John-Anthony Disotto), TechRadar

item.title: Trump says TikTok deal is in the works. Here's where things stand with the company
item.description: In less than a month, TikTok could have one or a few new owners, be banned again, or simply receive another reprieve to continue operating in the United States.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: South Dakota pushes labels for political deepfake videos ahead of elections
item.description: South Dakota lawmakers have approved legislation that would impose criminal and civil penalties on those who try to use unlabeled deepfakes to influence voters in the state, with the law now awaiting signature from Gov. Larry Rhoden.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, The Hill

item.title: Observe links end-user experience with back-end troubleshooting
item.description: Observe has bolstered its observability platform with frontend monitoring capabilities that it says will enable developers and IT teams to gain visibility into application performance on end-user browsers and mobile applications.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Word for Microsoft 365 cheat sheet
item.description: Word has always been the workhorse app of the Microsoft Office suite. Nearly everyone who uses Office ends up using Word at some point, whether it be for writing memos, typing up agendas, creating reports, crafting business correspondence or any of a thousand other uses.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Computer owners duped in antivirus scam to get more than $25M
item.description: More than $25 million in payments will go out this week to customers who were tricked into paying for fraudulent tech support offered by two companies.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, NewsNation

item.title: Weaviate adds agents to its tech stack to ease gen AI app development
item.description: Vector database provider Weaviate has added three new agents to its development stack to facilitate the development of generative AI-based applications. These agents are modular agentic workflows that use large language models (LLMs) and prompts to interact dynamically with data in Weaviate.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Elon Musk claims X being targeted in 'massive cyberattack' as service goes down
item.description: Hours after a series of outages Monday that left X unavailable to thousands of users, Elon Musk claimed that the social media platform was being targeted in a “massive cyberattack.” “We get attacked every day, but this was done with a lot of resources," Musk claimed in a post.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE CHAPMAN and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: ServiceNow to pay $2.85B for Moveworks’ AI tools
item.description: ServiceNow announced plans to purchase Moveworks, its front-end AI assistant and enterprise search technology for $2.85 billion in cash and stock.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Fortran, Delphi rise in Tiobe popularity index
item.description: Older programming languages such as Fortran and Delphi are making a comeback, based on the results of this month’s Tiobe index of language popularity. Tiobe attributes their rise to the need to maintain vital legacy systems still dependent on them.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Musk outlines DOGE work in rare TV interview
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk offered new details about the work of President Trump’s Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) on Monday, while doubling down on the group’s efforts amid mounting criticism.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Voice-cloning companies hit for lack of safeguards against scammers
item.description: Companies creating generative AI (genAI) tools that can clone voices are not doing enough to prevent scammers from using them to defraud customers, according to a study from Consumer Reports.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Is that iPhone app spying? Apple’s App Privacy Report reveals all
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson urges you to use Apple’s App Privacy Report to see what your apps are really up to.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Transferring data from old laptop to new one
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson talks about how to smoothly transfer your data from an old laptop to a new one using cloud storage or external drives.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The True Value of Open Source in Tech
item.description: The true value of open source in tech, from AI to business value
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Building generative AI? Get ready for generative UI
item.description: There’s currently no stopping generative AI spending. Analyst firm Omdia expects spending on generative AI applications to hit $58 billion by 2028. Yet while this level of investment is a significant amount, actual deployment of generative AI applications is still slow.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Siemens announces $285 million investment in US manufacturing
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Siemens plans to invest $285 million in manufacturing in the United States, the tech company said Thursday, including two new facilities in California and Texas. The U.S. is the largest market for the company. The recent investments in the Siemens' U.S.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Utah becomes the first state to pass legislation requiring app stores to verify ages
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) — Utah on Wednesday became the first state to pass legislation requiring app stores to verify users’ ages and get parental consent for minors to download apps to their devices. The bill headed to the desk of Gov.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News
